title: Everything we announced at Plaid Effects 2026
text: Plaid announced new AI models, fraud tools, lending products, payments capabilities, and developer tools at Effects 2026.

title: Plaid Guaranteed Payments: Approve more transactions without the risk
text: Introducing Plaid Guaranteed Payments, a fully managed solution that guarantees approved ACH transactions, backed by Plaid’s fraud and risk intelligence.

title: Ti3 is here: A bigger graph for a fast moving fraud landscape
text: Trust Index 3, the latest model powering Plaid Protect, helps fraud teams detect coordinated attacks with deeper network intelligence.

title: What ChatGPT’s new experience signals for digital finance
text: OpenAI’s new features in ChatGPT, powered by Plaid, show how AI and trusted financial data can deliver more personalized digital finance experiences.

title: Bank Intelligence is expanding for financial institutions
text: Plaid expands Bank Intelligence with Fraud Insights and Primacy Score, giving institutions real-time fraud signals and a clearer view of customer loyalty.

title: What states should know before submitting their Medicaid verification plan
text: CMS is asking states to rethink Medicaid provider verification. Here’s what strong fraud prevention plans should include beyond credential checks.

title: Introducing Plaid Cash Advance Index for smarter decisioning
text: Meet Plaid Cash Advance Index, a new way to assess repayment risk using network insights and cash flow data to improve approvals and reduce delinquency.

title: Meet the new engine behind Plaid Income
text: Plaid Income’s new engine improves income classification, adds granular taxonomy, and gives teams more control over how income is calculated.

title: Build personalized finance apps in Replit with Plaid
text: Plaid’s native Replit connector lets anyone build custom finance apps fast. Turn your financial data into AI tools for budgeting, insights, and investing.

title: Product Updates – April 2026
text: Explore Plaid’s April 2026 updates: new User APIs, margin balances in Investments, automated partner alerts, and smarter payment rules.

title: Agencies Announce Dollar Thresholds for Applicability of Truth in Lending and Consumer Leasing Rules for Consumer Credit and Lease Transactions

title: Agencies Announce Dollar Thresholds for Smaller Loan Exemption from Appraisal Requirements for Higher-priced Mortgage Loans
text: The CFPB, FRB, and OCC are announcing that the 2026 threshold for higher-priced mortgage loans that are subject to special appraisal requirements will increase from $33,500 to $34,200.

title: CFPB’s Supervision Division Releases New 'Humility Pledge'
text: Washington, D.C.—Today, the CFPB made changes to how the Supervision Division conducts exams. Previously, under the leadership of Director Chopra and Biden’s Director of Supervision Lorelei Salas, a former Soros activist who was put on leave in February 2025, this division was the weaponized arm of the CFPB.

title: CFPB Notifies Court it Cannot Lawfully Draw Funds from the Federal Reserve
text: Washington, D.C.—Today, the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) filed a notice informing the court in NTEU v. Vought that the Department of Justice’s Office of Legal Counsel (OLC) has determined that the Bureau may not legally request funds at this time from the Federal Reserve under Dodd-Frank.

title: CFPB Reaches Settlement with FirstCash, Inc. and Its Subsidiaries for Military Lending Act Violations
text: Today, in the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s (CFPB) November 12, 2021 lawsuit against FirstCash, Inc., and nineteen subsidiaries alleging violations of the Military Lending Act (MLA), the parties reached a settlement and jointly filed a stipulated final judgment and proposed order, which if entered by the court, would resolve the lawsuit.

title: CFPB Amends Wise Order for Remittance Practices
text: The CFPB amended its January 30, 2025 consent order with the international remittance company Wise resolving claims including advertising inaccurate fees and failing to properly disclose exchange rates and other costs.

title: CFPB Announcement Regarding Enforcement Actions Related to Buy Now, Pay Later Loans
text: The CFPB is announcing that it will not prioritize enforcement actions taken on the basis of the Truth in Lending (Regulation Z); Use of Digital User Accounts to Access Buy Now, Pay Later Loans, 89 Fed. Reg. 47,068 (May 31, 2024) (“Buy Now, Pay Later”).

title: Circle co-founder raises $30m for agentic finance startup Catena
text: Agentic finance startup Catena Labs has raised $30 million in Series A financing and applied for a national trust bank charter.

title: Tether to build national stablecoin for Georgian Government
text: Tether has been commissioned by the Government of Georgia to develop GEL₮, a stablecoin representing the Georgian Lari.

title: UK Government commissions review into bank branch closures
text: The UK Government has commissioned an independent review to examine the impact of bank branch closures and consider whether further intervention is needed to protect access to services.

title: SmartComply bids to help UK firms reopen African payment corridors
text: Lagos-based compliance and cybersecurity company SmartComply has launched in the UK, making its AI-powered anti-money laundering platform available to British payment firms serving African markets.

title: SME banking platform Relay raises $50m
text: Relay, a business banking and money management service for SMEs, has secured $50 million in growth investment from General Catalyst.

title: 'The clock is ticking' - ECB calls in banks over Mythos risks
text: The European Central Bank has called in banks for a meeting this week to urge them to speed up their efforts to fix IT issues exposed by new AI models.

title: Italian state lender to raise stake in Nexi
text: Italy’s state lender Cassa Depositi e Presti is set to increase its stake Nexi, strengthening its hold on the strategically important payments giant, which has attracted takeover interest from US private equity group CVC.

title: Mastercard asks Brazilian payment processors to split Will losses
text: Mastercard is asking Brazilian payment processors to absorb some of nearly $1 billion in losses connected to the failure of Banco Masters.

title: TD builds AI model to speed up mortgage applications
text: Canada's TD Bank has launched an agentic AI model that automates and streamlines the application process for mortgages and home equity lines of credit.

title: Wealthtech Farther raises $150m
text: Wealth management platform Farther has hit unicorn status after raising $150 million in Series D funding led by General Atlantic.
